feat: rendering optimisation pipeline (#1306, #1307, #1308, #1309) (#1310)

* feat: add render instrumentation and strategy comparison

Add RenderInstrumentation class to capture per-render metrics:
- Total render time (Stopwatch-based)
- PipeWriter GetSpan/FlushAsync call counts
- Fragment copy count
- Allocation delta via GC.GetAllocatedBytesForCurrentThread

Add InstrumentedPipeWriter wrapper that transparently counts
PipeWriter operations without adding hot-path allocations.

Add RenderBenchmark utility for single-shot and averaged
multi-iteration benchmarking of render strategies.

Add docs/render-strategy-comparison.md documenting 4 evaluated
strategies (UTF-8 Fragment, Arena, Compiled, Template-with-holes)
with recommendation for Strategy D.

9 new tests covering all instrumentation counters, snapshot,
reset, data integrity, and benchmark averaging.

Closes #1306

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* feat: template-with-holes render architecture

Implement precompiled template execution pipeline:

- RenderSegment struct: static UTF-8 byte fragments or dynamic
  field index placeholders (holes) with raw-HTML flag
- RenderPlan: flat array of segments with field key mapping and
  precomputed static byte count for size estimation
- TemplatePlanCompiler: parses template strings at startup,
  pre-encodes static text to UTF-8 bytes, deduplicates field
  indices for repeated keys, handles malformed tokens gracefully
- TemplatePlanExecutor: linear segment iteration with page-key
  precedence over app-key resolution, direct PipeWriter output,
  HTML encoding for safety (html_ prefix bypasses encoding)

Zero allocations in the render hot path — all compilation
happens at startup, execution is sequential copy + format.

26 new tests covering compilation, execution, key precedence,
HTML encoding, raw HTML, repeated keys, UTF-8, and scale.

Closes #1307

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* feat: SIMD-accelerated fragment copy for renderer

Add SimdCopy.CopyFragment using Vector128 (portable across x64
SSE2 and ARM64 NEON/AdvSIMD) for fragments >= 16 bytes:
- Main loop processes 16 bytes per iteration via vector
  load/store
- Tail bytes handled with Unsafe.CopyBlockUnaligned
- Fragments < 16 bytes fall back to Span.CopyTo

Integrate SIMD copy into TemplatePlanExecutor.WriteFragment
so all static HTML fragments in compiled render plans use
vectorised memory operations.

11 new tests covering: empty, 1-byte, 15-byte (fallback),
16-byte (exact vector), 17-byte (vector + tail), 20-byte,
32-byte, 256-byte, typical HTML fragment size, and end-to-end
PipeWriter integration.

Closes #1308

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* feat: single-span render pipeline for minimal PipeWriter overhead

Add SingleSpanRenderer that precomputes response size and renders
the entire output into a single contiguous buffer:
- EstimateTotalSize: sums static fragment sizes + dynamic value
  estimates with HTML entity expansion headroom
- RenderToSpan: writes all segments into a pre-allocated Span<byte>
  using SIMD fragment copy + inline HTML encoding, tracking offset
  with a single integer
- RenderAsync: single GetSpan(totalSize) → render → single
  Advance(bytesWritten) → single FlushAsync

Verified via InstrumentedPipeWriter: exactly 1 GetSpan, 1 Advance,
1 FlushAsync per render — compared to O(segments) calls in the
multi-write TemplatePlanExecutor.

15 new tests covering substitution, key precedence, HTML encoding,
raw HTML, UTF-8, size estimation, span rendering, instrumented
single-flush verification, and comparison with multi-write renderer.

Closes #1309

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

perf: pre-build Flutter in CI, map grouping, cursor pagination & lazy city profiles (#94)

## Summary

Three improvements rolled into one branch:

### 1. Pre-built Flutter CI + slim Docker image
- Flutter is built in CI via `subosito/flutter-action`, artifact is
passed to Docker
- Docker image reduced to nginx-only (`nginx:stable-alpine`) — no
Flutter SDK at runtime
- `nginx.conf` added: `Cache-Control: no-store` on `index.html` /
`flutter_service_worker.js`, SPA fallback for deep links
- `entrypoint.sh` injects `API_BASE_URL` at container start via `sed` so
the image is environment-agnostic

### 2. Startup crash fix
- PR #91 removed `DbManager` from providers but `AppLoadingPage` still
called `context.read<DbManager>()` → blank screen on launch
- Removed stale `DbManager` dependency from `AppLoadingManager` and
`AppLoadingPage`

### 3. Map location grouping, cursor pagination & lazy city profiles
- **`MapGateway` / `MapGatewayImpl`** — new gateway calling `GET
/profile/map`; returns alumni pre-grouped by city with coordinates
resolved server-side (no more fetching all profiles for map pins)
- **`MapRepositoryImpl`** — replaced bulk `getAllUsers()` with
`MapGateway.getMapLocations()`; event-only cities still resolve
coordinates via `LocationsRepository`
- **`PaginatedResult<T>`** — generic freezed model mirroring backend
`Paginated<T>` schema
- **`MapLocationGroup`** — freezed model for a single map pin (country,
city, lat, lng, count)
- **`UsersGateway` / `UsersRepositoryImpl`** — `getAllUsers()` now
returns `PaginatedResult<Profile>` with cursor support; added
`getUsersAtLocation(location)` for city-scoped pages
- **`CityDataPage`** — lazily loads profiles when user opens "In city",
with "Load more" for subsequent pages
- **`app.dart`** — wires `MapGatewayImpl` into DI
- **`Dockerfile`** — `dart run build_runner build` added before `flutter
build web` for freezed code generation

### Related backend PR
Depends on `iu-alumni/iu-alumni-backend#70`

---------

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: register /profile/map route before /{user_id} to prevent shadowing (#74)

The dynamic route GET /profile/{user_id} was registered before the
static route GET /profile/map, causing FastAPI to match /profile/map
with user_id="map" and return 404 "User not found".

Move map.router to be included before get_other_profile.router so the
static /map path takes precedence.

commit.message:
feat: numeric route ID dispatch for O(1) array-indexed routing (#1311)

- RouteHandlerData gets RouteId (ushort) + RouteKey, auto-assigned at RegisterRoute()
- Dense RouteHandlerData[] array indexed by route ID, rebuilt when routes change
- Numeric dispatch fast-path: if path starts with digit, branchless ASCII parse → array[id]
- ParseRouteId: AggressiveInlining, overflow guard, stops at non-digit
- Invalid route IDs return 404 immediately (no fallthrough to string router)
- GET /bmw/routes metadata endpoint: JSON array of {id, verb, path, params}
- /bmw/ paths and numeric paths bypass setup redirect
- 155 routes with dense IDs, all reachable by number
- Backwards compatible: non-digit paths use existing 3-tier router
- Zero string allocations on numeric dispatch path
- AOT-safe: no reflection, no dynamic dispatch

Verified: GET /6 → healthz 200, GET /9999 → 404, /bmw/routes → 155 routes
All 178 tests pass.

## Description

devices in the system, including remote (ETH-connected) chips, not just
PCIe-connected ones.

Users who need to restrict which devices are visible can use the
`TT_VISIBLE_DEVICES` environment variable, which is already respected by
the PCIe enumeration layer inside topology discovery.

## List of the changes

**Use `TopologyDiscovery::discover()` instead of
`PCIDevice::enumerate_devices()`:**
- Replaces direct PCIe device enumeration with topology discovery, which
also finds ETH-connected remote chips.
- Devices are now identified by logical `ChipId` instead of PCIe device
ID.

**Remove `--devices` CLI option:**
- The option for filtering by PCIe device IDs is removed. Use
`TT_VISIBLE_DEVICES` to control which devices are visible.

## Testing
Manual

fix: PostgreSQL compatibility - knex insert().returning('id') pattern

- Fix all routes to use .returning('id') for cross-DB insert compatibility
  (incidents, users, sla, notifications, monitoring, service-catalog)
- Fix tokenService.js password reset token insert

commit.message:
Fix Grid column title not visible when column is in a group (#690)

In paintColumnHeaderWithGroup(), the 'width' variable was used for both
the accumulated group width and the individual column width. After the
while loop accumulated widths of all columns in the group, the inflated
value was incorrectly passed to the column header renderer's setBounds(),
causing right-aligned column titles and sort arrows to not be visible.

Introduce a separate 'groupWidth' variable for the group header width
accumulation, keeping 'width' as the individual column width.

Clarify skillpm’s position relative to GitHub CLI/Claude plugin specs (#57)

Users asked what emerging GitHub CLI and Claude plugin specs imply for
skillpm. This PR clarifies that those specs are host/runtime extension
mechanisms, while skillpm remains focused on portable Agent Skills
packaging and orchestration.

- **Documentation: plugin-spec positioning**
  - Added a new section to `README.md`:
    - `How this relates to plugin specs (GitHub CLI / Claude Code)`
  - Added a matching section to `docs/index.md`:
    - `How this relates to plugin specs`
  - Both sections explicitly state:
    - plugin specs and Agent Skills are complementary, not replacements
- skillpm’s model is unchanged (`npm` for deps + `skills` for wiring +
`add-mcp` for MCP config)

- **Docs consistency**
- Kept language aligned across repository README and docs homepage so
users get the same answer regardless of entry point.

Example excerpt added:

```md
- Plugin specs define host-specific extension/runtime hooks.
- Agent Skills define portable instruction packages that can be reused across agent systems.

For skillpm, the model stays the same: npm for dependency management, plus `skills` and `add-mcp` for wiring and MCP configuration.
```

commit.message:
feat: add profit-cascade strategy with 12-tier dynamic profit targeting

- ChanceManager: cycles 50%→40%→25%→20%→14%→10%→5%→2%→1%→0.5%→0.1%→0.01%
- BetManager: bet_size = (balance × target_pct) / payout_multiplier
- RiskManager: safe mode at -20% drawdown (0.3% bets at 50% until full recovery)
- ProfitTracker: tracks bets, wins, losses, net profit, peak balance
- MoonshotManager: every 500 bets fires 3% stake at random 0.5/0.1/0.01% chance
- Loss recovery: +12% per loss (chance ≥ 5%), +5% per loss (chance < 5%)
- Win: advance tier + recalculate bet; attempts exhausted: advance tier anyway
- Registers as 'profit-cascade' via plugin decorator

* Fix help-element blur: use clip-path spotlight on backdrop (#945)

* Initial plan

* Fix help-element blur: use clip-path spotlight on backdrop instead of z-index

The backdrop-filter: blur(4px) on the CDK overlay backdrop blurs everything
behind it. Since the help element lives in the regular DOM (inside app-root)
while the backdrop is in the CDK overlay container (a separate stacking
context), z-index alone cannot position the help element above the backdrop.

Fix: Apply a clip-path with an evenodd polygon to the backdrop element,
cutting a rectangular hole over the help element. This keeps the blur effect
on everything else while leaving the help element crisp and unblurred,
creating a spotlight effect.

Remove TinyXML2 dependency and refactor XML handling (#22)

* Remove TinyXML2 dependency and refactor XML handling

Eliminated all usage of TinyXML2 from the build system and source code. XML parsing and construction for subblock metadata is now handled via libCZI's internal helpers and direct string manipulation. fix(pipeline): auth bypass + tags array + busboy dep + ruvector retry HYD-11

- dashboard.js: allow x-api-key header to bypass session auth middleware so
  machine-to-machine calls (plaud-sync, agents) work without a browser session
- dashboard.js: fix tags passed as JSON.stringify() string instead of array[]
  to addAudioTranscript() causing tags.join() TypeError and 500 on every ingest
- dashboard.js: remove now-redundant API key check inside /api/ingest/audio
- package.json: add busboy@1.6.0 dep (was missing, causing 500 on multipart parse)
- ruvectorStore.js: retry init up to 5x with 2s/4s/6s/8s backoff on lock errors
  so cross-process DB lock clears before giving up and falling back to LanceDB

Result: whisper transcription + LanceDB ingest confirmed working end-to-end
